comment: Quality Gates catalog -- perf dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/perf/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/perf/. v0.12.0 ships file-relocate stubs only; full gate logic deferred to v0.12.1 per MIGRATE-03 (all rows WAIVED until 2026-07-26).

asserts: the bench output (markdown table) is greppable from docs/benchmarks/latency.md
[v0.12.1] the latency cells in docs/benchmarks/latency.md match the bench output to within +/-2ms

rows.owner_hint: if RED: quality/gates/perf/latency-bench.sh regressed (D-CONV-3, 2026-07-04: the pre-migration scripts/latency-bench.sh shim this owner_hint used to describe as 'not yet migrated' was deleted -- the migration referenced here completed back in Wave E); WAIVED until 2026-09-15

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): still sim-derived numbers only, per R2 § A.2 -- bench runs (sim), but the real-backend headline cross-check remains a self-declared stub. Full perf-dimension implementation (latency cross-check + token-economy cross-check + headline-numbers cross-check) remains out of v0.13.0 REQUIREMENTS.md scope. The 'v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P97 (Good-to-haves polish + milestone close, launch-readiness slot) -- perf-dimension full implementation

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the sim-derived latency bench regressed, `bash quality/gates/perf/latency-bench.sh sim` would exit non-zero or the docs/benchmarks/latency.md table would go missing; a real-backend headline drift would NOT be caught today because that cross-check is the explicitly-deferred, still-stubbed half of this row's claim.

asserts: python3 quality/gates/perf/bench_token_economy.py --offline exits 0 with NO ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set (JSONL-usage methodology, P115 amendment #10)
the headline four-axis medians (output-token / cache-create / total-input-context / cost reduction) are greppable from docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md

the doc is regenerated deterministically + offline from the committed benchmarks/captures/*.json live-GitHub session-usage records (median-of-3 per arm, 2 arms), NOT from the Anthropic count_tokens endpoint
[un-waive, future code phase per 115-UNWAIVE-PATH.md] bench_token_economy.py asserts the measured output-token reduction (~94%) instead of returning 0 unconditionally

rows.owner_hint: if RED: the bench no longer regenerates docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md deterministically from benchmarks/captures/*.json, or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY leaked back into the headline path; WAIVED until 2026-09-15 pending the headline assertion (see 115-UNWAIVE-PATH.md, T6)

waiver.reason: P115 T5 (2026-07-16) switched the headline to the JSONL session-usage methodology (amendment #10): the doc is now regenerated deterministically + offline (no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) from the committed benchmarks/captures/*.json live-GitHub session records (median-of-3 x 2 arms, task 'read 3 issues, edit 1, push' against reubenjohn/reposix). The bench REGENERATES + emits the four-axis medians but still returns 0 unconditionally -- it does not yet ASSERT the ~94% output-token reduction, so a WRONG-but-present headline would not fail the gate. Un-waive requires adding that assertion; the precise value + assertion site are documented in 115-UNWAIVE-PATH.md (T6 documents the path; a future code phase implements it). Plan Task 6 explicitly assigns the un-waive to that path, so T5 rewrites the contract text but leaves the waiver active.
waiver.dimension_owner: v0.15.0 P115 T5 -- see 115-UNWAIVE-PATH.md (T6 owns the un-waive path)
waiver.tracked_in: P115 T6 (115-UNWAIVE-PATH.md) -> a future code phase adds the headline assertion

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the captures-derived token-economy regen broke, `python3 quality/gates/perf/bench_token_economy.py --offline` would exit non-zero (e.g. missing/invalid benchmarks/captures/*.json) or the docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md four-axis table would go missing; a WRONG-but-present headline percentage would NOT be caught because main() still returns 0 for any computed value -- that assertion is the still-deferred half of this row's claim (un-waive path: 115-UNWAIVE-PATH.md).

asserts: [v0.12.1] every headline number in docs/index.md + README.md is greppable from a benchmark fixture file
[v0.12.1] the cross-check fails RED if any headline drifts from its bench source

rows.owner_hint: if RED: full v0.13.x implementation; the verifier compares headline numbers in docs/index.md + README.md against benchmark fixture files -- WAIVED until 2026-09-15. NOTE (P90 90-05 R2 § A.2/H): the verifier script quality/gates/perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py is confirmed ABSENT entirely from quality/gates/perf/ -- this is a dangling-verifier row, flagged in quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 1, not just a soft-target row.

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04) + dangling-verifier flag: quality/gates/perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py confirmed absent (R2 § A.2/H). Full perf-dimension implementation remains out of v0.13.0 REQUIREMENTS.md scope. The 'v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03' label was dead -- repointed per D90-02(c) mass-renewal rule.
waiver.tracked_in: P97 (Good-to-haves polish + milestone close, launch-readiness slot) -- perf-dimension full implementation incl. this row's missing verifier script

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If a headline number drifted from its benchmark source, the cross-check script would need to grep docs/index.md + README.md against the fixture files and exit non-zero on mismatch; since quality/gates/perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py is confirmed absent, no such comparison can run today and drift would go undetected.

rows._provenance_note: Hand-edit per documented gap (NOT Principle A): reposix-quality bind only supports the docs-alignment dimension at v0.13.0; perf dim mints stay hand-edited until GOOD-TO-HAVES-01 ships the verb extension. See .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md § GOOD-TO-HAVES-01.

asserts: bash quality/gates/perf/list-call-count.sh exits 0 against the local cargo workspace
with N=200 records seeded in wiremock and a one-record edit pushed, the helper makes >=1 list_changed_since REST call AND zero list_records REST calls (cursor-present hot path)
the positive-control sibling test confirms wiremock fails RED if expect(0) on list_records is reverted

rows.owner_hint: if RED: crates/reposix-remote/src/precheck.rs regressed OR handle_export reverted to unconditional list_records walk; check stderr for whether wiremock saw a list_records call | D-CONV-1 (2026-07-04): stripped from pre-pr and demoted to on-demand -- the underlying test (crates/reposix-remote/tests/perf_l1.rs::l1_precheck_uses_list_changed_since_not_list_records) is already exercised by ci.yml's `test` job (`cargo test --workspace --locked` runs every workspace test including this one); kept on-demand for standalone focused verification with this specific invariant's PASS/FAIL semantics.
